NOTICE OF MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE FILED BY PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY HEARING SET FOR APR-22-2014 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, THE MOTION FOR ORDER TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED, AND THE ORDER DEEMING MATTERS ADMITTED. SEE SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THESE DOCUMENTS ARE NOT SCANNED ON THE COURT'S REGISTER OF ACTIONS. IN FACT, THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS DOES NOT SHOW THAT THE JUDGE PRO TEM EVER EXECUTED A WRITTEN ORDER DEEMING THE MATTERS ADMITTED. THE COURT CANNOT GRANT JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS BASED ON THE PRESENT RECORD BEFORE IT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-22-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jill Bates-Moore, Esq., Tel. 510.985.9250, appearing specially for the plaintiff;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied without prejudice. The moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the Requests for Admissions, the Motion for Order to Deem Matters Admitted, and the order deeming matters admitted. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. These documents are not scanned on the Court's register of actions. In fact, the register of actions does not show that the Judge Pro Tem ever executed a written order deeming the matters admitted. The Court cannot grant judgment on the pleadings based on the present record before it. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff UNIFUND CCR, LLC'S unopposed Motion for Order to Deem Matters Admitted is GRANTED. The moving party shall recover $ 2,000 in fees. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Judge Pro Tem: James P. Watson; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Continued To Jul-08-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff UNIFUND CCR, LLC'S Motion for Order to Deem Matters Admitted is continued to July 8, 2013 to give Unifund CCR, LLC the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Unifund CCR, LLC must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 19, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
